title: Sour Chewy Peruvian Fish Stew
description: A vibrant onepot Peruvian fish stew with a tangy limetamarind broth, a splash of Grand Marnier, and a quick broil for a caramelized finish - Whole30friendly and irresistibly chewy.

ingredients.name: Grand Marnier
ingredients.notes: non‑alcoholic version allowed for Whole30, otherwise keep a small splash of the original

instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic; sauté until onion is translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the diced bell pepper, cumin, paprika, and cayenne; cook for another minute to toast the spices.
Add the tamarind paste and fish stock to the pot, stirring to combine; bring to a gentle simmer.
Gently fold in the fish cubes, reducing the heat to low. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Simmer uncovered for 10–12 minutes, or until the fish is opaque but still slightly firm (chewy).
Stir in the Grand Marnier and lime juice; simmer for an additional 2 minutes to meld flavors.
Transfer the stew to oven‑safe serving bowls, then broil in a preheated broiler for 2 minutes, or until the top is lightly caramelized.
Remove from the broiler, garnish with chopped cilantro and parsley, and serve immediately.
